data.title: Blue Owl Technology Finance Corp. announced Q4 2025 highlights: NII/share $0.26, dividends $0.40/share, NAV/share $17.33. New investments $2.3 billion, sales $881 million. Annualized dividend yield 9.2%.
data.description: Blue Owl Technology Finance Corp. reported its financial results for Q4 and year-end 2025. In Q4 2025, GAAP net investment income per share was $0.26, adjusted NII per share was $0.30, and dividends declared were $0.40 per share, with a 9.2% annualized yield. NAV per share increased to $17.33. New investments for the quarter were $2.3 billion, while sales/repayments were $881 million. Net debt-to-equity ratio was 0.75x. The company repurchased $64.6 million of stock and approved a $300 million repurchase program. Portfolio had investments in 199 companies valued at $14.3 billion. In 2025, new investment commitments were $5.6 billion, with $4.3 billion funded, and sales/repayments of $3.1 billion. For Q4, new commitments were $2.3 billion, with $1.7 billion funded, and sales/repayments of $881 million. The company's weighted average yield was 9.6% for accruing debt. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: CRH completes share buyback, returns $0.3 billion to shareholders, repurchases 2.2 million shares. Total returns now $9.7 billion.
data.description: CRH, a major supplier of construction materials, has successfully finished the recent stage of its share repurchase initiative, giving back an additional $0.3 billion to investors. From Nov. 6, 2025, to Feb. 17, 2026, 2.2 million common shares traded on the NYSE were bought back. This marks a total of $9.7 billion returned to shareholders through the continued buyback plan that began in May 201.

data.title: Blue Owl Capital Corp (NYSE: OBDC) released Q4 2025 results: $0.38 NII per share, $0.36 adj NII per share, $684M new investment commitments, $1.4B sales/repayments, $148M stock repurchased at 86% price-to-book value, $1.4B of investments sold, and $0.37 dividend per share declared. Portfolio valued at $16.5B across 234 companies.(nav: $14.81/share). CEO cites strong earnings and credit performance.
data.description: Blue Owl Capital Corporation (OBDC) reported its financial results for Q4 and the year 2025, highlighting a GAAP net investment income per share of $0.38 and adjusted NII per share of $0.36 for Q4. Dividends declared were $0.37 per share, with an annualized yield of 10.0%. New investment commitments for Q4 were $684 million, with sales and repayments totaling $1.4 billion. NAV per share was $14.81. OBDC repurchased $148 million of common stock and approved a $300 million share repurchase program. Moody's upgraded OBDC to Baa2. OBDC and other Blue Owl BDCs sold $1.4 billion in investments. Portfolio composition featured investments in 234 companies across various sectors totaling $16.5 billion at fair value. New investment commitments for the year totaled $4.3 billion. Stock repurchases totaled $148 million, and a new repurchase program of $300 million was approved. The asset sale involved selling $1.4 billion of investments at fair value with a purchase price of 99.8% of par value. Dividends of $0.37 per share were declared for Q1 2026.

data.title: Carvana's Q4 earnings beat estimates with $5.6B revenue, $4.22 EPS. 163,522 vehicles sold, revenue up 58%. ADS and margins down.
data.description: Carvana Co (NYSE: CVNA) released its fourth-quarter financial results, exceeding analyst expectations. The company reported a revenue of $5.60 billion, surpassing the estimated $5.26 billion. Earnings per share for the quarter were $4.22. Carvana saw a significant 58% increase in total revenue year-over-year, attributed to the sale of 163,522 vehicles in Q4, marking a 43% rise in vehicle sales compared to the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA stood at $511 million with a margin of 9.1%. Despite some decline in Adjusted EBITDA and margins, Carvana stock is steadily advancing following the announcement.

data.title: Clearwater Paper Corp reports 2025 full year results: Net sales $1.6B (up 12% from 2024), net loss $53M.
data.description: In Spokane, Washington, Clearwater Paper Corporation (NYSE: CLW), a leading supplier of bleached paperboard to North American converters, announced its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ending on December 31, 2025. Throughout 2025, the company achieved significant milestones, including a 12% increase in net sales totaling $1.6 billion compared to the previous year. This growth in sales was driven by a 14% rise in volumes, primarily attributed to the full-year operation of the Augusta facility. However, the company reported a net loss from continuing operations amounting to $53 million, equivalent to $3.28 per diluted share.
